title: Experimented using GPT ATLAS like a quant copilot to spin up and backtest a bunch of strats. Results were… interesting
author: Proper_Suggestion830
selftext:
Spent the afternoon seeing how far GPT Atlas could go as a quant assistant.

I connected it to a trading research workspace Lona and just talked to it like  
Create a Donchian breakout strategy on AAPL with stop-loss 3% and take-profit 6%.

It generated the logic, executed a backtest, and spit out metrics:  
• Total return +2.20 %  
• Max drawdown 3.8 %  
• Sharpe –0.85  
• Win rate 45 %

I then iterated in this & asked it things like improve Sharpe or add RSI filter & Atlas basically became an interactive quant coach, summarising results, suggesting tweaks, tracking different reports automatically.

Here’s what I tested so far:  
• RSI mean-reversion (+1.18 % return, 87 % win rate)  
• Bollinger Bands + RSI (+7.4 %)  
• MACD crossover (+4.5 %)  
• Donchian breakout (+2.2 %)

Not crazy profits (obviously over-fit risk), but the *process* of going from prompt → Python → backtest → report in seconds was cool.

comments.author: Humanless_ai
comments.body: Nice, think we’ll see more stuff like this built straight into ai trading sandboxes soon

replies.author: Proper_Suggestion830
replies.body: Lets see, gonna keep playing around and try to get some better results

comments.author: GlitchWL
comments.body: That's interesting! I eventually want to do something like that for WealthLab, any tips on connecting Atlas to the workstation product?
